summary.intent: Address Codex automated reviewer findings on PR #732 in fogodev/ars-ui, fix the flagged issues, push fixes, resolve threads, and retrigger review until Codex approves.
summary.outcome: Two rounds of Codex findings were addressed: a third commit fixed Tailwind spacing to exclude disabled closable rows and injected Tabs CSS into both widget example crates. Both fixes were pushed, threads resolved, and a new review pass triggered.

repo: Dioxus SSR escapes '&' as '&' rather than '&', diverging from Leptos escaping — test assertions must be written against selector semantics, not raw HTML entity strings.
Tailwind spacing for closable rows must gate on both data-ars-closable presence AND absence of data-ars-disabled.
CSS widget examples (widgets-leptos-css, widgets-dioxus-css) require explicit STYLES injection in their category roots for .ars-tabs* selectors to be exercised in tests.
Codex review is triggered via '@codex review' PR comment and tracked via 👀 reaction + unresolved thread count using GitHub GraphQL API.
Dioxus CloseTrigger hooks must be computed unconditionally with only the rsx gated, to avoid hook ordering violations.

code.finding: Tailwind shell spacing classes updated to require data-ars-closable and exclude data-ars-disabled rows.
Same Tailwind spacing fix applied to Dioxus variant.
STYLES injection added so Tabs CSS template is included in the CSS widget example bundle.
Same STYLES injection added for Dioxus CSS widget example.
Test assertions relaxed to check class selector semantics instead of exact HTML entity encoding to handle Dioxus '&' vs '&' divergence.

workflow: Codex review state is reliably tracked by combining reaction presence (👀) with unresolved thread count via GraphQL — both signals together confirm active vs finished review.
Polling at 5-minute intervals with reaction + thread-count checks is sufficient cadence for Codex review passes that typically complete in 15-30 minutes.
cargo check on example crates is a fast compile verification step before running full tests.
cargo xfmt should be run before every commit to avoid formatting-only follow-up commits.

friction: Dioxus SSR HTML entity encoding differs from Leptos, causing test assertions written for Leptos to fail on Dioxus — required a second patch to relax assertions.
Codex review passes take 15-30 minutes with no intermediate progress signal beyond the 👀 reaction, requiring sustained polling.
Three separate commit/push/resolve/retrigger cycles were needed across the session, each with overhead from hooks and GraphQL thread resolution.
open_items: Codex may produce additional findings in subsequent review passes — the session ended after retriggering, not after receiving approval.
The Dioxus SSR entity encoding divergence from Leptos is a systemic issue that could affect other test assertions beyond tabs — worth a broader audit.
